description_long: This object is a dual-unit, industrial-style radio receiver with a robust rectangular body, predominantly colored in yellow with black and metallic accents. The front face features multiple dials, switches, and ports, as well as two prominent speaker grilles and a frequency indicator panel. The top surface has two large, dark access panels or covers, along with ventilation holes for heat dissipation. A long, thin metallic antenna extends vertically from the center, and there are additional detachable or foldable components visible on the sides. The overall construction suggests a heavy-duty, possibly military or field-use radio, with ruggedized corners and reinforced edges. The labeling and markings on the front panel include some non-English text and a logo, indicating a specific brand or manufacturer.
description: A rugged, dual-unit yellow radio receiver with multiple dials, speaker grilles, and a central antenna, designed for heavy-duty or field use. The front panel features detailed controls, ports, and a frequency indicator.
description_view_0: The left side view shows the rear panel with two large circular connectors, a central switch, and visible ventilation holes on the top.
description_view_1: The front view highlights the main control interface, including dials, switches, speaker grilles, a frequency indicator, and the brand logo.
description_view_2: The right side view mirrors the left, showing similar connectors and ventilation, with the antenna visible extending upward.
description_view_3: The rear view displays the back panels with additional ventilation grilles and two large circular features, likely for power or signal connections.
